get.description: Audit view over the trace graph. Returns recent traces with principal/key/time/kind filters.

post.description: Phase 7.5 — Spectron-as-agent chat endpoint. Stores the user message via /facts, retrieves context via the unified /query router, calls the configured LLM provider, stores the assistant response via /facts, and emits a response_trace. Set `stream=true` for SSE.
Phase 11b: pool recent facts and consolidate into observations
Query memory and return formatted context for LLM prompt injection

get.description: Lists Layer 0 knowledge documents

post.description: Upload a new Layer 0 knowledge document (multipart/form-data)

403.description: Requested scope outside the caller's write region

409.description: Label count exceeds the per-fact cap

get.description: Lists keywords across the Context

post.description: HNSW vector search over keyword embeddings

get.description: Gets a keyword and its linked documents

post.description: Runs a top-k semantic query over the Layer 0 chunks
Phase 6c: recompute doc-to-doc semantic_similarity edges across the Context. Returns the number of edges emitted (existing edges are retained — the (in, out, kind) triple is unique).

get.description: Gets a Layer 0 knowledge document by id

put.description: Replaces a Layer 0 knowledge document and re-runs its pipeline

get.description: Lists chunks for a document
Lists keywords linked to a single document
Streams the raw bytes of a document

post.description: Phase 11a: elaborate links for a single entity or sweep low-relation entities

get.description: List entities (optionally filtered by type), ordered by type then name. Unpaginated by default; pass `limit` (capped at 500) and `offset` to page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max entities to return (default: all, capped at 500)

get.description: Get a single entity with its current attributes and relations

delete.description: Soft-delete an entity and all its current attributes and relations

get.description: Get the full attribute history (supersession chain) for an entity key

post.description: Write a single fact. Dispatches on `infer` mode: full (LLM-extracted), triples (caller-supplied), preview (dry run), none (literal).

409.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different body

post.description: Bulk conversation ingest. Persists each message as a turn and routes extraction through `reconcile_extraction`.

409.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different body

post.description: Semantic forget: finds matching memories by similarity and soft-deletes them
Memory hygiene sweep (duplicates / contradictions / injection / unscoped content)

get.description: Resolve an inspect ref (entity, attribute, relation, passage, or trace) to a normalised view. Ref grammar: `entity:<type>/<name>`, `attribute:<type>/<name>/<key>`, `relation:<src_type>/<src_name>-[<label>]-><dst_type>/<dst_name>`, `passage:<document_id>/<index>`, `trace:<id>`.

parameters.name: asOf
parameters.description: Tri-temporal: read as-of this transaction time

parameters.name: atInstant
parameters.description: Tri-temporal: read at this valid time

get.description: List the caller's own data-plane keys (id, name, timestamps, attenuating grants). Never returns the secret. Gated by the Context's `allow_self_service_keys` flag.

post.description: Mint a new data-plane API key bound to the caller's own principal. The optional `grants` body only attenuates (never widens) the caller's effective grants. Gated by the Context's `allow_self_service_keys` flag; rejects legacy / delegated callers.

parameters.name: ttlSeconds
parameters.description:
Seconds until the minted/rotated key expires. Omitted on create =
"never expires" (subject to the Context's `max_token_ttl_seconds` cap).
Omitted on rotate = inherit the key's current expiry.

403.description: Self-service keys disabled or ineligible caller

409.description: Key name already taken in the Context

delete.description: Delete one of the caller's own keys by name. A name that does not belong to the caller surfaces as 404 (no cross-principal leakage). Gated by `allow_self_service_keys`; rejects legacy / delegated callers.

403.description: Self-service keys disabled or ineligible caller

404.description: Key does not exist or is not owned by the caller

post.description: Rotate the secret on one of the caller's own keys. The id stays stable (preserving the principal binding and attenuating grants); the previous secret stops validating immediately. `ttlSeconds` is reset-or-inherit, matching the management rotate path.

parameters.name: ttlSeconds
parameters.description:
Seconds until the minted/rotated key expires. Omitted on create =
"never expires" (subject to the Context's `max_token_ttl_seconds` cap).
Omitted on rotate = inherit the key's current expiry.

403.description: Self-service keys disabled or ineligible caller

404.description: Key does not exist or is not owned by the caller

post.description: Apply periodic importance decay across all memories
Run category-driven expiry sweep for context-category memories

get.description: Return the calling token's resolved identity, the principal's grants, the key's attenuating grants (if any), and the effective per-verb grants. Self-only; never requires `grant:manage`.
List the Context's principals (requires the `grant:manage` grant)
Fetch one principal by id (requires the `grant:manage` grant)
List the verbs a principal effectively holds at a scope path (requires `grant:manage` over the path)

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Scope path to resolve effective verbs at

parameters.name: asOf
parameters.description: RFC 3339 instant: resolve against grant history at that time instead of current grants

post.description: Grant a scope pattern to a principal for the given verbs (requires `grant:manage` over the pattern)

403.description: Caller lacks `grant:manage` over the region, or the target is the immutable `system` principal

delete.description: Revoke a scope pattern from a principal for the given verbs (requires `grant:manage` over the pattern)

403.description: Caller lacks `grant:manage` over the region, or the target is the immutable `system` principal

get.description: Get a profile suitable for LLM system prompt injection

post.description: Phase 7 unified query: four-tier router + fused ranker over the substrate
Reflect on stored memories with LLM synthesis
Grant scope patterns to a principal (not implemented — surface locked)

get.description: List the Context's registered scope nodes (live and tombstoned). Only nodes the caller has a grant over are returned (`scope:read`, or any other verb in the region); a `grant:manage` holder over `/` sees the whole tree.

post.description: Register a scope node (auto-vivifies missing ancestors; enforces scope:create, depth, node-budget, and per-node policy).

delete.description: Tombstone a scope node (soft-delete: sets tombstoned_at = time::now())

post.description: Erase everything tagged under a scope subtree (GDPR forget). Requires the `memory:forget` grant over the subtree.
Create a new conversation session

delete.description: Delete a session and all its turns

post.description: Retrieve session context for LLM prompt injection

get.description: List turns in a session, oldest first. Paginated: `limit` defaults to 100 and is capped at 500; use `offset` to page.
Get the full structured memory state grouped by category
List recent query traces
Get aggregate query trace statistics

Get a single query trace by ID, with its one-hop neighborhood: the owning response trace and the retrieval traces that fed it, including their returned targets. Accepts both decision-trace ids and chat response-trace ids.

ApiErrorResponse.description: Unified error response type for all REST endpoints.

bypassCache.description:
Phase 7.6 — when `true` skips the tier-2 response cache and
forces a fresh tier-3 call. Defaults to `false`.

labels.description: Descriptive labels for rows the chat persists.

scopes.description:
DNF scope selector for the conversation, e.g. `[["org/apple"]]` or co-owned
`["team/a","team/b"]`. Tags the rows the chat persists and seeds the session.

labels.description:
Label filter (design §4): `key=value` strings the result rows must all
carry; applied after the scope predicate (never widening).

lens.description:
Read **lens** (DNF: OR of conjunctive clauses) narrowing the effective read
region (design §7.1); see [`QueryMemoryRequestJson::lens`]. Empty = the
whole granted region.

scopes.description:
DNF scope selector for the session, e.g. `[["org/apple"]]` or co-owned
`["team/a","team/b"]`. Each clause's nodes must lie within the caller's
write region; empty ⇒ the caller's default write region.

DecisionKind.description:
Bounded enum for the writer to dispatch on. Serializes as the
`create` / `update` / `supersede` wire strings so it can stand in for
the public `/consolidate` response `kind` field.

DocGeoFilterJson.description:
Phase 6.5 — geographic filter on `/documents/query`. Mirrors the
shape on the memory `/query` endpoint.

DocumentStatus.description:
Lifecycle state of a [`Document`] in the ingestion pipeline.

Matches the SurrealQL `ASSERT $value IN [...]` on `document.status` in
`V1__schema.surql`. The `Keywording` state is reserved for
Phase C; in Phase A the pipeline transitions straight from `Embedding` to
`Ready`.

DocumentUploadForm.description:
OpenAPI documentation shape for the `multipart/form-data` upload/reprocess
body: a `file` part plus a `metadata` JSON part ([`UploadMetadataJson`], which
carries `scopes` / `labels` / `title` / …). The handlers parse the multipart
stream directly; this type exists only so the parts (and `scopes`) appear in
the generated spec. The `metadata` part MUST precede `file` on the wire.

metadata.description: The document metadata part, sent as a serialised JSON object.

asOf.description:
Echoes the `as_of` instant the verbs were resolved at, when the request
asked for a historical view; absent for a current-grants response.

path.description: The scope path the verbs were resolved at (canonical form).

verbs.description: The verbs the principal effectively holds at `path`.

labels.description:
Descriptive labels for the persisted rows (accepted + recorded; row
application deferred).

scopes.description: Scope paths the batch targets (canonical slash-paths, e.g. `org/apple/`).

labels.description:
Descriptive labels for the persisted rows (scope-model §5). Accepted
and recorded on the wire now; row-level application lands in a
follow-up (cf. `ScopeView` — accepted but behaves as `Strict`).

oneOf.description: Override the memory category applied to extracted/triples facts.

observed_at.description:
Optional RFC3339 known/observed time for the written facts (backfill).
When set, the facts are stamped with this `created_at` (known time), so
`as_of` queries see them dated to when they were observed rather than to
wall-clock ingest. `None` keeps the default (facts dated to ingest time).

oneOf.description: Role to record on the turn (`user` by default).

scopes.description:
Scope paths the write targets — canonical slash-paths, e.g. `org/apple/`
(scope-model §5). Used when auto-creating a session. Empty = the
caller's default write region.

session_id.description:
Existing session id to attach the turn to. When absent, the
handler auto-creates a session before the write.

text.description:
Free-form text (used by `full`, `preview`, `none`). Optional only
when `infer = triples` is supplied.

dryRun.description:
When `true`, the handler computes and returns the would-be deletion
count (`deleted`) without writing anything — the preview side of a
preview-then-confirm flow. When omitted or `false`, matching memories
are soft-deleted immediately.

purge.description:
Phase 10.7 — right-to-be-forgotten. When `true`, the handler
also removes the supersession history (rows with `valid_until`
set) that match the same scope/entity criteria. When omitted or
`false`, prior rows are retained for audit even after the
current row is soft-deleted.

forgotten.description: Number of fact rows erased across the scoped tables.

GeoFilterJson.description:
Wire shape for the Phase 6.5 geo filter on `/query`. Mirrors the
in-process [`crate::memory::types::GeoFilter`] enum.

oneOf.description: Within `radius_km` of a point. Mutually exclusive with `within`.

within.description: Inside a WKT polygon. Mutually exclusive with `near`.

GrantRequestJson.description:
Body for `POST` / `DELETE /{ctx}/principals/{id}/grants` — the scope pattern
and the verbs to grant/revoke it for.

verbs.description:
Grant verbs to apply: any of `memory:read|memory:write|memory:forget|
scope:read|scope:create|scope:delete|grant:manage`.

Grants.description: Per-verb scope pattern map. Keys are grant verbs in `<noun>:<verb>` form (`memory:read`, `memory:write`, `memory:forget`, `scope:read`, `scope:create`, `scope:delete`, `grant:manage`); values are arrays of scope patterns.

GraphEdgeKind.description: Graph edge kinds used by [`QueryRequest::graph_edges`] for HybridGraph re-ranking.

InferMode.description:
Inference mode for the `/facts` write API.

Decides which extraction path runs and which provenance kind the
reconciler tags the writes with (see the per-variant docs below).

InjectionKind.description:
The detector taxonomy — the closed set of injection-pattern
categories the scanner can emit. Serialises to `snake_case` wire
strings (`instruction_override`, `system_prompt_leak`, …) on the
`/fsck` response and in the persisted `uncertainty.source.kinds`
audit payload, consistent with the rest of the API's enums. Because
the pattern table is keyed by this enum (not `&'static str`), adding a
detector without a matching variant is a compile error.

oneOf.description:
The currently-live attribute for this `(entity, key)` pair, if
any. `None` once every assertion has been superseded or forgotten.

history.description:
Full supersession chain, newest-first. Includes `current` when
present.

matches.description:
Live relations matching the `src -[label]-> dst` shape. Empty if
none survive the temporal filter.

KeyDetailJson.description: One row in `GET /{ctx}/keys`. Bearer secret and stored hash never surface.

oneOf.description:
The key's attenuating grants, when set. Absent ⇒ inherits the
principal's grants.

lastUsedAt.description: Last successful validation; absent if the key has never been used.

validUntil.description: Mint-time expiry. Absent ⇒ no expiry was set.

Label.description: A metadata label in the form `key=value`. Keys are soft-declared; values are free.

MemoryCategory.description: Memory category classification applied during extraction.

MemoryQueryMode.description:
Retrieval mode hint. The default is `Hybrid`, which engages the
full fused ranker. The other modes are kept as escape hatches so
callers can debug a single signal in isolation.

MintKeyBody.description:
Request body for `POST /{ctx}/keys`. Every field is optional — an empty
body mints a key inheriting the caller's grants with no expiry, named
after the random key id.

grants.description:
Optional attenuating per-verb grant map. Every requested pattern must
be ⊆ the caller's effective region for that verb (fail-closed); an
out-of-range pattern returns 400. Absent ⇒ inherit principal grants.

name.description:
Optional human-readable name (unique per Context). Auto-derived from
the minted key id when absent.

MintedKeyJson.description:
`POST /{ctx}/keys` response. The plaintext key is returned **once** and
never persisted in this shape — the caller must store it themselves.

id.description: The key's stable record-id key (survives rotation).

key.description:
The full bearer key (`sp-{id}-{secret}`). Send as
`Authorization: Bearer …`.

QueryKind.description: What kind of question the query represents. Drives tier selection.

asOf.description:
Phase 11.5 — historical query timestamp (known time). When set
the retrieval path walks the supersession chain to surface the
row that was current as-of this instant.

atInstant.description:
Phase 11.5 — system-time query. Prepends `VERSION d'…'` to the
underlying `SELECT` so SurrealDB MVCC returns the substrate
state at the supplied instant.

include.description:
Phase 7 — which families of result to include. Defaults to
`["facts", "passages"]` (both). Pass either alone to scope the
response.

includeDuplicates.description:
When `false` (the default), chunks flagged as near-duplicates of an older
chunk are excluded from the fused ranker's chunk recall so the same text
does not occupy several ranks. Set `true` to include them (parity with the
documents `/query` opt-in).

labels.description:
Label filter (design §4): `key=value` strings the result rows must **all**
carry. Applied after the scope predicate — labels never widen access, only
narrow the already-authorised result set. Empty = no label filter.

lens.description:
Scope/permission model — read **lens**, a DNF selector (OR of conjunctive
clauses): each clause is an AND of scope paths / `/*` subtree patterns, and
a row is kept if it involves every pattern of some clause (clauses are
OR'd). Narrows the caller's effective read region for this query (design
§7.1); empty = the whole granted region. The lens can only narrow —
permission gating from the caller's grants always applies on top, so an
out-of-region lens yields empty results, never a 403.

oneOf.description:
Phase 6.5 — optional geographic filter applied at read time
over `entity.location` and `memory_chunk.source.location`. See
[`GeoFilterJson`] for the accepted shape.

scopeView.description:
Scope read breadth: `strict` (default) | `merged` | `crossTeam`. Only
`strict` is enforced pre-launch; `merged` / `crossTeam` are accepted and
behave as `strict` until their region logic ships (design §7.1).

source.description:
Phase 7 — free-form source label. Recorded on the trace for
audit replay; doesn't affect retrieval today.

validFrom.description:
Phase 11.5 — valid-time (world-time) lower bound. Filters rows
whose `valid_from` is ≥ this timestamp.

validUntil.description:
Phase 11.5 — valid-time (world-time) upper bound. Filters rows
whose `valid_until` is ≤ this timestamp.

classificationKind.description:
Phase 7 — query-understanding output. `kind` is one of
`direct_lookup` / `hybrid` / `full_context`; `seed_entities`
is the (possibly empty) set of entity surface forms extracted
from the query.

trace.description: Phase 7 — short trace summary returned inline.

QueryMode.description: Chunk query mode: vector-only, BM25-only, hybrid RRF, or hybrid plus graph-density re-rank.

decomposeQuery.description:
W5b: opt into sub-question decomposition for this query. The
caller fans out to one retrieval per sub-question and
RRF-merges the result lists. Like `use_hyde`, requires the
Context to have an LLM provider attached.

oneOf.description:
Phase 6.5 — optional geographic filter applied at read time
over `chunk.location`.

useHyde.description:
W5a: opt into HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) for this
query. Requires the Context to have an LLM provider attached;
callers without one should leave this `false`. Defaults to
`false` so existing agents see the same vector-only behaviour.

useReranker.description:
W5c: opt into cross-encoder reranking of the top-K. Requires
the server to have a `Reranker` provider attached. Off by
default; takes effect after the store returns results so it
composes naturally with HyDE / decomposition / hybrid graph.

traceId.description:
Id of the trace cluster written during the reflect, for correlation
with `/traces/{id}`. Empty when no trace was written (#229).

RegisterScopeRequestJson.description:
Body for `POST /{context}/scopes` — the scope path plus optional display
metadata. Governance (value policy / `max_children`) is operator-only and
lives on the management API, so it is not accepted here.

displayName.description: Optional display name for the UI.

path.description:
Scope path to register, e.g. `org/apple/product/ipad` (a trailing `/` is
accepted and canonicalised).

ResponseTraceSummaryJson.description: Summary of the response trace that owns a decision trace.

ResultKind.description:
Coarse-grained result type. Mirrors the row table the hit lives
in.

RetrievalTraceSummaryJson.description: Summary of one retrieval trace paired with a decision trace.

returned.description:
The targets this retrieval returned, ranked. Empty for legacy traces
that persisted only the `retrievedIds` array.

label.description: A short human label for the target.

ref.description:
An inspect ref for the target (`attribute:<type>/<name>/<key>`,
`entity:<type>/<name>`, `passage:<document_id>/<index>`), when the
target kind has one.

ScopeForgetRequestJson.description: Body for `POST /{context}/scopes/forget` — the scope subtree to erase.

path.description: Scope path whose subtree should be forgotten, e.g. `user/stu/`.

ScopeGrantRequestJson.description:
Body for the `POST /{context}/scope-grants` stub. Locks the request
shape (subject principal + verb → patterns) without implementing the
grant-event write.

grants.description: Grant verb → scope patterns to grant.

ScopeNodeJson.description: One scope node in the `GET /{context}/scopes` response.

path.description: Canonical scope path (plain slashes, trailing `/`; `/` for root).

tombstonedAt.description: Soft-delete timestamp; `null` while the node is live.

ScopePath.description: A validated, canonical scope path in slash form, e.g. `org/apple/product/ipad/`. Root is `/`.

ScopePattern.description: A scope pattern: an exact node (`org/apple/product/ipad`) or a subtree (`org/apple/*` / `/*`).

ScopeSets.description: A DNF scope selector: an OR of conjunctive clauses. Each clause is an array of scope paths, ALL of which a reader must cover (AND); the outer array is the OR. E.g. [["team/a"],["team/b","clearance/secret"]] means team/a OR (team/b AND clearance/secret). Empty means unscoped (the caller's default region). A bare string is also accepted as a singleton clause.

SourceKindCountJson.description: §15 operational signal (#174): one provenance source-kind + its count.

SourceRefJson.description: Compact provenance ref carried on attribute/relation detail JSON.

ref.description:
Compact navigable ref — `turn:<id>`, `doc:<id>`, or `trace:<id>` —
when the source carries one. Document refs carry no passage index
(only the passage record id is stored).

sessionId.description: The session the source turn belongs to (`turn` kind only).

Tier.description:
Which tier resolved the query. Exposed on the response for the
CLI's `--tier` flag (Phase 8) and so callers can tell when their
query went all the way to tier 4.

TraceKind.description:
The trace `kind` discriminant — a closed three-value set over the
trace tables. Serialises to `decision` / `retrieval` / `response`
so the `/audit` wire shape and the `kind=` filter stay locked to the
enum and can't drift (typos are rejected, the match arms are
exhaustive, and the OpenAPI schema enumerates the allowed values).

oneOf.description:
The owning response trace (chat / reflect / the legacy query persist).
Populated on single-trace reads only; absent from `/traces` lists.

retrievals.description:
The retrieval traces that fed this decision, with their returned
targets. Populated on single-trace reads only; absent from `/traces`
lists.

responseTracesCached.description:
Phase 7.6 — subset with `reused_from IS NOT NONE` (served from
the entity-aware cache).

responseTracesTotal.description: Phase 7.6 — `response_trace` rows in the configured window.

sourceKindDistribution.description:
§15 operational signals (#174), aggregated over the same window:
distribution of attribute provenance by `source.kind`.

windowHours.description:
Phase 7.6 — window over which the response-trace counts were
aggregated.

Triple.description:
A structured triple supplied directly by the caller (no LLM).

`key` + `value` create an attribute; if `value` is `None` and `target`
is `Some`, the triple represents a relation edge from `entity` to
`target` with `key` as the relation label.

TurnRole.description: Role of a conversation turn participant.

labels.description:
Optional `"key=value"` labels stamped onto the document and inherited by its
chunks/sections (validated like facts: `_`-prefixed keys rejected with 400, a
per-fact count cap with 409). Reconciled graph rows are never labelled.

observedAt.description:
Optional RFC3339 known/observed time for the content (backfill). Stamped as the
known-time of facts derived from this document, so `as_of` queries see them
dated to when the content was observed. Omitted means facts date to ingest time.

scopes.description:
Optional DNF scope selector tagging the document, e.g.
`[["org/apple/product/macbook"]]` (or co-owned `["team/a","team/b"]`). Each
clause's nodes must lie within the caller's `memory:write` region (a request
outside it is a 403). Omitted ⇒ the document inherits the caller's whole
write region (unchanged behaviour). Clients must send the `metadata` part
before the `file` part for it to apply.

Verb.description: A grant verb in `<noun>:<verb>` form: one of `memory:read`, `memory:write`, `memory:forget`, `scope:read`, `scope:create`, `scope:delete`, `grant:manage`.

WhoamiJson.description:
The `GET /{ctx}/me` response. Reflects only the *calling* token's resolved
identity — no principal lookup gated by `grant:manage`.

delegatedPrincipalId.description:
When the token is delegating (`X-Spectron-On-Behalf-Of`), the target
principal id; absent for non-delegated calls.

effectiveGrants.description:
Effective per-verb grants after attenuation + delegation. This is what
gates every read/write — useful for clients pre-flighting writes.

grants.description: The principal's authoritative per-verb grant map.

principalId.description:
The token's principal id. Always a bound principal — an unbound key is
rejected at authentication, so this is never a bare key id.

oneOf.description:
The calling key's attenuating grants, when set. Absent ⇒ the key
inherits the principal's grants wholesale.
